title: eremophila saligna
intro: Eremophila saligna, also known as Willow Emu Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its grey-green foliage and clusters of blue or purple flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine and has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eremophila saligna include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully explore its medicinal applications and health benefits.
